title: Nuclear reactor maker X-Energy valued at $11.9 billion in stellar Nasdaq debut
description: X-Energy shares surged 30.9% in their Nasdaq debut, fetching the company a valuation of $11.9 billion and signaling strong interest in the developers of carbon-free energy that has ​the potential to fuel the unprecedented boom in artificial intelligence.
content:
The Amazon-backed nuclear reactor developer raised $1.02 billion in its ‌initial public offering on Thursday by selling 44.3 million shares in its upsized offering at $23 apiece. The stock opened at $30.11.

title: Intel forecasts second-quarter revenue above estimates, shares jump 19%

title: Meta plans 10% layoffs as AI spending soars

title: Google to invest up to $40 billion in AI rival Anthropic
description: Google-parent Alphabet will invest up to $40 billion in Anthropic, as the tech giant deepens ​its partnership with the artificial intelligence startup that is also its rival ‌in the global AI race.
content:

title: How Musk Is Staking Tesla’s Future on AI, Robotaxis and Robots
description: Tesla Inc. is among the most expensive stocks in the S&P 500. That might seem odd in the wake of one of its worst quarters for electric vehicle sales in years. But it’s not EVs that are keeping the shares trading at more than 180 times expected earnings. Instead, investors are betting on what Chief Executive Officer Elon Musk sees as the company’s true calling: self-driving cars and humanoid robots, powered by artificial intelligence.
